reasoning: Mikie Sherrill is a strong favorite to win the 2025 New Jersey Gubernatorial Election. As the Democratic nominee in a predominantly blue state (nearly a 900,000-voter registration advantage), she benefits from significant structural advantages. Furthermore, assuming Trump won the 2024 election and is in the White House in 2025, historical trends highly favor the party out of power (Democrats) in NJ odd-year elections. Polling in October shows her generally leading Republican Jack Ciattarelli by solid margins (FDU +7, Quinnipiac ~+8, Rutgers +9), with only one recent poll (Emerson) indicating a tie. Sherrill also boasts a substantial advantage in both campaign cash-on-hand ($6.5M to $3.7M) and outside spending, including a massive $20M ad buy from the DGA. While polling errors in 2021 underestimated Ciattarelli, Sherrill's structural advantages, polling lead, and the expected favorable environment for Democrats in this off-year cycle make an upset relatively unlikely.
